items.title: What makes a star a star? A strange 'in‑between' celestial object is testing astronomers' boundaries
items.summary: A star called TOI-2155 lies around 1,350 light-years (839 trillion miles) from Earth. It is a little bigger, heavier and hotter than the sun, and it is not particularly interesting or unusual in itself.

items.title: Workplace depression is common. Managers can make it worse, or better
items.summary: Australia has a mental health crisis. The Productivity Commission has found mental health issues cost Australia up to A$200 billion to A$220 billion per year—one-tenth of annual economic output.

items.title: These glaciers are becoming critical climate havens as America's iconic mountain glaciers and their water diminish
items.summary: If you have ever hiked in the high peaks of Colorado, the Wasatch Range in Utah or the Tetons in Wyoming, you've almost certainly seen a rock glacier, perhaps without even knowing it.

items.summary: For more than a decade, residents of Austin's Colony, a neighborhood in an unincorporated area of southeast Austin outside the city service area, have voiced concern about their tap water's intermittent discoloration and…

items.title: Comet from another star has a composition unlike anything else in our solar system
items.summary: Astronomers have revealed new details about the makeup and age of a visiting comet that was born around a distant star. They conclude that the composition of 3I/Atlas is strikingly different from any object found in our …

items.title: Hot spell roasts eastern US as holiday weekend approaches
items.summary: Millions of Americans sweltered in stifling heat and humidity Thursday, with dangerous temperatures expected to hit swaths of densely populated areas through the Fourth of July holiday weekend.

items.title: Schools should teach children more about how money works
items.summary: I recently volunteered to teach some lessons in finance to pupils at a primary school. Over six sessions, I spoke to a group of 10- and 11-year-olds about things like value, savings, cost and risk.

items.title: Massive sturgeon once bred in Britain's rivers, boosting reintroduction hopes

items.title: Last-minute launch problem delays satellite rescue mission for NASA
items.summary: A rush rescue mission to save a NASA space telescope remains grounded, this time because of a last-minute launch problem.

items.title: How much do friends influence teens' mental health? What a new study can (and can't) tell us
items.summary: During adolescence, young people become especially sensitive to peer influence—more so than at any other time in life. So how does this affect their mental health?

items.title: Scourge of satellites lighting up the sky could be mitigated with help of ultra-black coating
items.summary: Astrophysicists working to tackle the growing impact of satellite constellations have pioneered a new ultra-black coating as one possible way to mitigate the problem.

items.title: Genomic tool can help measure resilience in Merino sheep
items.summary: A new resilience test for merino sheep is using hereditary markers to help producers identify which animals are better able to cope with stressors in their environment.

items.title: The natural history of every U.S. state is on display at a new D.C. exhibit
items.summary: The Smithsonian Natural History Museum’s latest exhibit, “From These Lands,” connects visitors with America’s natural history.

items.title: Abandoned farmland restored to wildflower meadow without sowing seeds
items.summary: Abandoned farmland can be transformed into wildflower-rich grassland habitat without the need for expensive and labor-intensive seeding, a new study by UCL researchers finds.

items.title: The animal behind most aggressive wildlife encounters may surprise you
items.summary: Analysis of 3,000 incidents in Canada reveals which animal–human activity combos are especially risky. Of note: Elk and campgrounds are a bad mix.
